subject: Fix health check action name
commit_message:
Fix health check action name

Fix name of the health check action which is sent to policy

Change-Id: I38536d2194963b58d1cf4b3e123142dddbfe9dd1
Issue-ID: CLAMP-296
Signed-off-by: adamwudzinski <adam.wudzinski@nokia.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

Is there really a space between Health and Check? I'm looking at the APPC documentation:
https://onap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/submodules/appc.git/docs/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide.html#lcm-commands

Or is this change simply a GUI display change?

Patch Set 1:

> Is there really a space between Health and Check? I'm looking at
 > the APPC documentation:
 > https://onap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/submodules/appc.git/docs/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide.html#lcm-commands
 > 
 > Or is this change simply a GUI display change?

 > Is there really a space between Health and Check? I'm looking at
 > the APPC documentation:
 > https://onap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/submodules/appc.git/docs/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide/APPC%20LCM%20API%20Guide.html#lcm-commands
 > 
 > Or is this change simply a GUI display change?

It's not a GUI change. This string is sent to Policy component, where it's transformed into a final action name that is sent to APP-C. The transformation happens in AppcLcmRecipeFormatter::getBodyRecipe method in Policy.

Patch Set 1:

Ok, but according to APPC documentation the correct syntax is "HealthCheck". No space. Unless I am missing something??

Patch Set 1:

> Ok, but according to APPC documentation the correct syntax is
 > "HealthCheck". No space. Unless I am missing something??

Yes, you are right, but that "Health Check" is not a value that is passed to APP-C. 
Let's assume that in the following entry:
'"Health-Check": "Health Check"'
"Health-Check" is a key, and "Health Check" is a value.
So if we talk about triggering APP-C, the key is important not the value,
because this key ("Health-Check") is first sent from CLAMP to Policy and then Policy dynamically creates APP-C action name from that. 

And from input "Health-Check" it will generate action name "Health Check", as APP-C requires.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Ok thanks for the explanation.

Patch Set 1:

> Ok thanks for the explanation.

You welcome :)
